{"body":"### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have searched [existing issues](https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3Abug) and this hasn't been reported yet
- [x] I am using the latest version of Moltis
- [ ] If this happened during a chat session, I included as much full session context as possible and redacted secrets

### What happened?

When using Mistral or Qwen models via "OpenAI Compatible" provider option, images send via Telegram can not be processed by the LLM.
Looking into [/crates/providers/src/lib.rs](https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/blob/main/crates/providers/src/lib.rs) unveils in function `pub fn supports_vision_for_model(model_id: &str) -> bool {...}` that there seams to be a static check for model name  prefixes to determine the models vision capability. Mistral und Qwen are not in that list, hence they are considered to be not vision compatible.

In reality modern models from both providers support vision: [Mistral Api](https://docs.mistral.ai/capabilities/vision) ,  [Qwen blog post](https://qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.5)

### Expected behavior

Almost all modern LLMs are multi modal and support vision.
Hence Moltis should consider all models by default to be vision compatible  and should only blacklist older models that don't support vision tasks.


### Steps to reproduce

Confirm that vision is not working for Mistral:
1. Add Mistral https://api.mistral.ai/v1 with the  "OpenAI Compatible"  option in the web panel and select a mistral model like mistral-medium-2505.
2.  Select the model for  your Telegram session in the web panel
2. Send an image via Telegram to Moltis and ask for its content 

Additional step to trick Moltis vision compatible check (optional)
1. Run a litellm api proxy with docker compose : 
```
version: '3.8'

services:
  litellm:
    image: ghcr.io/berriai/litellm:main-latest
    container_name: litellm
    ports:
      - "4000:4000"
    environment:
      - LITELLM_MASTER_KEY=${LITELLM_MASTER_KEY}
      - MISTRAL_API_KEY=${MISTRAL_API_KEY}
    volumes:
      - ./litellm-config.yaml:/app/config.yaml
    command: >
      --config /app/config.yaml
      --port 4000
```

2.  Spoof the model name in its litellm-config.yml to look like gpt-4o :   
```
model_list:
  - model_name: gpt-4o-mistral
    litellm_params:
      model: mistral/mistral-large-latest
      api_key: os.environ/MISTRAL_API_KEY
```

3. Add  litellm and the model with "OpenAI Compatible" 
4. Select the model gpt-4o-mistral for telegram session
5. Send an image via Telegram to Moltis and ask for its content 
6. Vision task work just fine with the spoofed model name because of its gpt-4o prefix

### Did this happen during a chat session?

No

### Chat session context (if applicable)

```markdown

```

### Error messages / logs

```shell

```

### Is this a regression?

No, this never worked

### Last working version

_No response_

### Moltis version

20260328.03

### Component

Gateway / Web UI

### Install method

Docker

### Operating system

Ubuntu / Debian

### Additional context

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture. Here's my analysis:

---

## Issue #556 Analysis

**The bug is confirmed.** `supports_vision_for_model()` at `crates/providers/src/lib.rs:776-808` uses a prefix-based allowlist that only recognizes: `claude-`, `gpt-4o`, `gpt-4-turbo`, `gpt-5`, `o3`/`o4`, `gemini-`, and `glm-*v*`. Mistral and Qwen models are not listed, so they default to `false`, causing images to be silently stripped rather than sent to the model.

The effect: when a user sends an image via Telegram to a Mistral or Qwen model, `tool_result_to_content()` in `runner.rs:728-732` sees `supports_vision=false` and calls `sanitize_tool_result()` which strips image data. The model never sees the image — a silent failure.

## Fix Plan

The reporter suggests inverting to default-true with a denylist. I think a **two-part approach** is better:

### Part 1: Expand the allowlist (immediate fix)
Add vision support for known vision-capable families:

| Prefix/Pattern | Models | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `pixtral` | Pixtral 12B, Pixtral Large | Mistral vision-specific |
| `mistral-large` | Mistral Large 2+ | Mistral docs confirm vision |
| `mistral-medium` | Mistral Medium | Mistral docs |
| `mistral-small` | Mistral Small 3+ | Mistral docs |
| `qwen` + contains `vl` | Qwen-VL, Qwen2-VL, Qwen2.5-VL, Qwen3-VL | Qwen blog |
| `deepseek` + contains `vl` | DeepSeek-VL | Common pattern |
| `llama` + contains `vision` | Llama vision variants | Meta docs |

### Part 2: Flip default for unknown models to `true`
The key insight from the reporter: the failure mode of **wrongly assuming vision** (API error — visible, diagnosable) is far better than **wrongly denying vision** (images silently stripped — invisible, confusing). 

Instead of the current default-deny at the bottom:
```rust
// Current: Default: no vision support  
false
```

Change to default-allow with a denylist of known text-only models:
```rust
// Known text-only models
if model_id.starts_with("codestral") { return false; }
if model_id.starts_with("kimi-") { return false; }
// GLM text-only (non-v suffix)
if model_id.starts_with("glm-") && !model_id.contains('v') { return false; }
// Default: assume vision support (API error is better than silent image stripping)
true
```

### Test updates needed
- Remove `assert!(!supports_vision_for_model("mistral-large-latest"))` — it's wrong (Mistral Large supports vision)
- Remove `assert!(!supports_vision_for_model("some-unknown-model"))` — default changes to true
- Add positive assertions for Mistral/Qwen vision models
- Keep negative assertions for codestral, kimi, glm text-only

### Files to modify
1. **`crates/providers/src/lib.rs`** — `supports_vision_for_model()` function and its tests
2. **`crates/providers/src/lib.rs`** — Update `MISTRAL_MODELS` list if we want to surface more Mistral models

Want me to implement this fix?

content.text: Now let me try closing the beads issue one more time.

Dolt server is persistently down. The commit is done — the beads issue can be closed once Dolt recovers.

**Summary of changes** (`crates/providers/src/lib.rs`):

**`supports_vision_for_model()`** — flipped from allowlist to denylist:
- **Before**: Only `claude-`, `gpt-4o`, `gpt-4-turbo`, `gpt-5`, `o3`/`o4`, `gemini-`, `glm-*v*` returned `true`. Everything else (including Mistral, Qwen, DeepSeek, etc.) was `false`.
- **After**: Only known text-only models return `false`:
  - `codestral*` (code-focused)
  - `gpt-3.5*`, `text-*`, `gpt-4-*` (legacy OpenAI, except `gpt-4-turbo`)
  - `glm-*` without `v` suffix (Z.AI text-only)
- Everything else defaults to `true` — Mistral, Qwen, Kimi, unknown models all get vision support.

**Rationale**: Sending images to a text-only model causes a visible API error. Silently stripping images from a vision-capable model is an invisible failure that confuses users. Default-true is the safer default.

**Tests**: 12 vision tests pass, including new assertions for Mistral/Qwen models and the default-true behavior.
